name: Will my claim get denied if I didn't always wear earplugs?

mainEntity.name: Will my claim get denied if I didn't always wear earplugs?

acceptedAnswer.description:
**Not necessarily; employer duty to provide protection and safe conditions often outweighs inconsistent use if negligence proven.**

Claims succeed if noise exceeded OSHA limits and employer failed duties.[1][3] Partial compliance doesn't bar recovery.

**Key factors:**
- Proof employer didn't supply or enforce protection.
- Your exposure history vs. protection availability.
- Severity of loss from cumulative noise.
- No gross negligence on your part.

Document what was provided; attorney navigates defenses.[2] Many win despite imperfect use.

Focus on employer faults.
